topic: Why I avoided posting videos on LinkedIn for 2 years

interview.speaker: interviewer
interview.text: When you think about hitting record on a video, what is the very first feeling that comes up?
interview.reason: Need to surface the raw emotional barrier before exploring specifics.

interview.speaker: user
interview.text: Honestly, a kind of dread. I know I will hate how I sound.

interview.speaker: interviewer
interview.text: Is it more about how you sound, or what people will think of what you actually say?
interview.reason: Isolating which fear is dominant — voice insecurity vs judgment of ideas.

interview.speaker: user
interview.text: Mostly what people will think. I worry my ideas are not polished enough.

interview.speaker: interviewer
interview.text: Has there been a moment when that fear stopped you from posting something you had already prepared?
interview.reason: Looking for a concrete story to anchor the fear.

interview.speaker: user
interview.text: Yes. I recorded a 90-second video for our product launch. Watched it back three times and deleted it.

interview.speaker: interviewer
interview.text: What was going through your head as you watched it back?
interview.reason: Need the inner monologue — the self-talk that drives avoidance.

interview.speaker: user
interview.text: I kept thinking: this is not good enough. Other people post perfectly scripted things and I look like I am rambling.

interview.speaker: interviewer
interview.text: What eventually made you decide to start posting anyway?
interview.reason: Need the turning point for a positive arc — what shifted the calculus.

interview.speaker: user
interview.text: A founder I respect posted a rough video and got ten times the engagement of their polished ones. I realised the rawness was the point.
